description:
You are acting as a lone developer who is tidying up the versioning information of a small project that already lives in your home directory.

Project layout that already exists when you start:

/home/user/project
└── (files)
    ├── VERSION
    └── CHANGELOG.md

1. VERSION  
   • Plain-text file containing a single line with the current semantic version (in the form MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, ending with a single newline).  
   • Current content before you start:  
     1.2.3⏎

2. CHANGELOG.md  
   • Markdown file with the project’s existing change history.  
   • Current content before you start:

     # Changelog

     ## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-12  
     ### Added  
     - Initial release.  

Objective
---------
A) Increment (bump) the **patch** version by exactly one (1.2.3 → 1.2.4).  
B) Write the new version (only the string, followed by a newline) back to /home/user/project/VERSION.  
C) Append a new entry to the bottom of /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md that documents this bump.  
D) Produce a small machine-readable log file documenting the change.

Required output artefacts
-------------------------
After you have finished, automated tests will verify the following three artefacts:

1. /home/user/project/VERSION  
   • Must contain exactly:  
     1.2.4⏎  
   • There must be **no leading/trailing spaces** and only **one final newline**.

2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  
   • Must still start with the original lines shown above.  
   • A new block must be added *under* the previous content that is **byte-for-byte** exactly this five-line fragment (including the blank line after it):

     ## [1.2.4] - 2023-08-01  
     ### Changed  
     - Bumped patch version from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4.  

   • The fragment must be preceded by a single blank line and followed by a single blank line.  
   • No other text is allowed to be inserted or removed anywhere in the file.

3. /home/user/version_bump.log  
   • New file that must be created in /home/user (sibling of the project directory).  
   • Must contain exactly two lines (each ending with a newline) in key=value format:

     OLD_VERSION=1.2.3  
     NEW_VERSION=1.2.4  

   • No extra whitespace and no additional lines.

Constraints & hints
-------------------
• Stay entirely in your own account; do **not** use sudo.  
• Only simple shell utilities (cat, echo, printf, etc.) are needed; 3-4 commands are enough.  
• Do **not** delete or rename any existing files.  
• Make sure the new files keep default user-writeable permissions (do not attempt chmod 000 or similar).  
• Remember that automated grading is strict about exact byte-for-byte matches, newlines, and spacing.  
• You are free to use any editor or redirection method, but the resulting artefacts must match the specifications above.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):

Directory /home/user/project already exists.

File /home/user/project/VERSION initial content (byte-exact):  
"1.2.3\n"

File /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md initial content (byte-exact):
"# Changelog\n\n## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-12\n### Added\n- Initial release.\n"

No file called /home/user/version_bump.log is present.

Expected final state (after the agent finishes):

/home/user/project/VERSION must contain:  
"1.2.4\n"

/home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md must contain exactly (byte-for-byte):  
"# Changelog\n\n## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-12\n### Added\n- Initial release.\n\n## [1.2.4] - 2023-08-01\n### Changed\n- Bumped patch version from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4.\n\n"

(NOTE: Two consecutive newline characters between blocks and one trailing newline at the very end.)

/home/user/version_bump.log must exist with content:  
"OLD_VERSION=1.2.3\nNEW_VERSION=1.2.4\n"

File/directory ownership must remain user:user; default umask (644 for files, 755 for directories) is acceptable.

No other files may be created or modified.
